research_metadata.scope: Betting odds data sources for NCAAB and other sports

key_findings: ESPN core API (sports.core.api.espn.com) DOES provide odds data via undocumented endpoints
ESPN site API (site.api.espn.com) scoreboard endpoint includes odds in JSON responses
NO major sportsbooks (DraftKings, FanDuel, BetMGM, Caesars) offer official public odds APIs
Multiple GitHub projects successfully scrape DraftKings/FanDuel via unofficial APIs
The Odds API remains best paid option (500 credits/month free tier)
OddsPortal.com is most comprehensive free data source but requires scraping
executive_summary.recommendation: ESPN scoreboard API is already implemented and working. Enhance with ESPN core API odds endpoints for additional bookmaker coverage. Keep The Odds API as primary source within credit budget.

data_sources.citation: [1] ESPN Hidden API. "Sports Core API Odds Endpoints." GitHub, 2025. https://github.com/pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API

pros: Returns odds embedded in scoreboard data

data_sources.citation: [3] The Odds API. "Sports Odds API Documentation V4." TheOddsAPI.com, 2026. https://the-odds-api.com/liveapi/guides/v4/
cons: Paid upgrade required for heavy use

data_sources.citation: [4] DraftKings Undocumented API. "GitHub - agad495/DKscraPy." GitHub, 2024. https://github.com/agad495/DKscraPy
data_sources.data_format: json from API, HTML from scraping

usage_example.code:
from event_scraper import EventScraper
scraper = EventScraper()
scraper.scrape('www.draftkings.com/event/12345')
print(scraper.odds_df.head())

pros: Captures all markets (not just main lines)
Uses APIs, not fragile HTML parsing
cons: May require VPN for international books
Bot detection and IP bans possible
Rate limiting required to avoid detection

data_sources.citation: [8] BetMGM Unofficial Access. "The Odds API - BetMGM Coverage." TheOddsAPI.com, 2026. https://the-odds-api.com/
pros: BetMGM included in The Odds API free tier

data_sources.citation: [9] Caesars/William Hill. "The Odds API - Caesars Coverage." TheOddsAPI.com, 2026. https://the-odds-api.com/
data_sources.integration_note: Caesars acquired William Hill, odds powered by William Hill

data_sources.citation: [10] Bovada/BetOnline. "Medium - Scraping Data From Bovada." Medium, 2024. https://medium.com/@aaroncolesmith/scraping-data-from-bovada-96f2647fd152
cons: Offshore books (not legal in all states)
Less relevant for US legal market

data_sources.citation: [11] CBS Sports. "CBS Sports Odds Page." CBS Sports, 2026. https://www.cbssports.com/college-basketball/odds/
[12] Yahoo Sports. "Yahoo Sports Betting Odds." Yahoo Sports, 2026. https://sports.yahoo.com/betting/odds/
data_sources.availability: odds displayed on website (powered by BetMGM)
pros: Yahoo displays BetMGM odds on website
cons: Odds powered by BetMGM (already accessible via The Odds API)

common_patterns: Major sportsbooks do NOT offer official public APIs
Undocumented APIs exist for all major books (DraftKings, FanDuel, BetMGM, Caesars)
GitHub scrapers use a mix of HTML scraping and unofficial API calls
ESPN provides most comprehensive free odds via hidden APIs
Third-party aggregators (The Odds API, SportsDataIO) are most reliable paid options
Scrapers universally use rate limiting to avoid detection
OddsPortal is the de facto standard for historical odds data
best_practices: Use official APIs (The Odds API) within credit budgets
Leverage ESPN hidden APIs for free supplemental data
Implement fallback chains: API -> ESPN -> Cache
Rate limit all requests (2 req/sec for ESPN, 3 req/sec for scrapers)
Track API credits and usage to avoid overages
Store odds data locally to minimize repeat requests
Respect robots.txt and TOS to avoid bans
pitfalls: Undocumented APIs can break without warning
Scraping violates most sportsbook TOS (legal risk)
IP bans common on aggressive scraping
Bot detection (Cloudflare, reCAPTCHA) on many sites
Rate limits often not documented (trial and error)
Team name matching requires fuzzy logic
emerging_trends: ESPN expanding odds coverage post-ESPN BET launch
Playwright replacing Selenium for modern scraping
Serverless deployment (AWS Lambda) for scheduled scraping
Docker containers for reproducible scraper environments

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Daily NCAAB odds collection within 500 API calls/month budget
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: The Odds API (primary) + ESPN Site API (fallback) + Manual CSV (override)
implementation_recommendations.rationale: The Odds API provides highest reliability and multiple bookmakers. ESPN fills gaps when credit budget low. Manual CSV allows human verification of close games.
implementation_recommendations.implementation_notes: Already implemented in OddsOrchestrator. Continue using existing strategy.

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: OddsPortal scraper (OddsHarvester) OR purchase historical data from SportsDataIO
implementation_recommendations.rationale: OddsPortal has most comprehensive historical odds database (free). SportsDataIO offers paid historical API if budget allows.
implementation_recommendations.implementation_notes: One-time backfill, worth the scraping effort. Use AWS Lambda + proxy rotation. Store in local SQLite for permanent access.

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Real-time odds monitoring for live betting arbitrage
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Fastest updates come from scraping individual sportsbook APIs. The Odds API updates every 5-10 minutes (too slow for arb).
implementation_recommendations.implementation_notes: High-risk (TOS violations). Only implement if pursuing arbitrage strategy. Requires VPS and proxy rotation.

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Expand coverage to additional bookmakers beyond The Odds API
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Implement ESPN Core API odds endpoints with bookmaker IDs (DraftKings:41, FanDuel:58, BetMGM:45, Caesars:38, Bet365:2000)
implementation_recommendations.rationale: ESPN Core API provides free access to multiple bookmakers. Higher bookmaker count improves line shopping for CLV.
implementation_recommendations.implementation_notes: Create new ESPNCoreOddsProvider class mirroring ESPNOddsProvider architecture. Add to OddsOrchestrator fallback chain.

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Player prop odds for NCAAB (higher edge than main lines)
implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: DKscraPy-style scraper targeting DraftKings unofficial API
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Player props have highest inefficiency but not available via The Odds API free tier. DraftKings has best NCAAB prop coverage.
implementation_recommendations.implementation_notes: Requires reverse-engineering DraftKings API. High maintenance risk. Consider paid DraftKings API via OpticOdds if budget allows.

popular_solutions: The Odds API is industry standard for small-scale projects
OddsPortal scraping for historical data nearly universal
Playwright replacing Selenium for modern web automation
Pandas DataFrame as standard odds data structure
AWS Lambda for serverless scheduled scraping
controversial_topics: Legal/ethical implications of scraping sportsbook sites
Whether undocumented APIs constitute TOS violations
Offshore books (Bovada/BetOnline) vs US legal books
Free vs paid odds APIs (The Odds API free tier sufficient?)
IP rotation and proxy usage for scraping
expert_opinions: The Odds API creator: 'Free tier designed for hobbyists, not commercial use'
Sports betting researchers: 'Closing line value requires historical odds, necessitates scraping'
Legal experts: 'Scraping publicly displayed odds is legal, but violates TOS and may result in IP bans'
Data engineers: 'Undocumented APIs preferred over HTML scraping due to stability'
Sports bettors: 'DraftKings and FanDuel have sharpest lines, prioritize these books for odds data'

immediate_actions: Test ESPN Core API odds endpoints with NCAAB event IDs
Map ESPN bookmaker provider IDs to actual sportsbook names
Verify which bookmakers return NCAAB odds (may differ from NFL/NBA)
Implement ESPNCoreOddsProvider class if endpoints work
Add to OddsOrchestrator fallback chain after The Odds API
short_term_goals: Backfill historical odds for 2020-2025 seasons using OddsPortal scraper
Store historical odds in SQLite closing_odds table
Validate historical odds against known closing lines
Calculate CLV for all past predictions using backfilled odds
Re-run Gatekeeper validation with real closing line data
long_term_considerations: Evaluate paid tier for The Odds API if free credits insufficient
Consider OpticOdds or SportsDataIO for premium odds feeds
Explore player prop odds scraping post-March Madness 2026
Build live odds monitoring for in-game betting opportunities
Implement odds arbitrage scanner across multiple books
